prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='NaiveMovingAverage', seasonality_champion='ARIMA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.47) and extreme seasonality variance (0.83), indicating models capture patterns very differently. Early horizon specialists (rf, NaiveSeasonal) differ from late horizon specialists (ARIMA), suggesting temporal pattern divergence. The DBA combination method is recommended to handle this heterogeneity while leveraging each model's unique strengths in trend tracking and seasonal decomposition.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "NaiveMovingAverage",
  "seasonality_champion": "ARIMA",
  "overall_champion": "ARIMA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "rf", "late": "ARIMA"},
  "tier1_models": ["ARIMA", "FT_rf", "catboost", "CWT_svr", "DWT_rf", "FT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "THETA"],
  "tier2_models": ["ETS", "DWT_svr", "CWT_catboost", "CWT_rf", "NaiveSeasonal", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "NaiveMovingAverage", "ONLY_CWT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.4693,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.47) and extreme seasonality variance (0.83), indicating models capture patterns very differently. Early horizon specialists (rf, NaiveSeasonal) differ from late horizon specialists (ARIMA), suggesting temporal pattern divergence. The DBA combination method is recommended to handle this heterogeneity while leveraging each model's unique strengths in trend tracking and seasonal decomposition."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25", "best_per_horizon_by_validation"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.25}},
  "score_preset": "balanced",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.83) and model disagreement. n_unique_winners=11 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. With only n_windows=3, shrinkage=0.25 on inverse_rmse_weights provides stability. DBA handles phase-shifted predictions from early/late horizon specialists (rf vs ARIMA). Top-k mean with k=3 balances diversity and robustness."
}
raw.statistician:
{
  "add_names": ["exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0", "ade_dynamic_error_beta0.3_trim1.0", "robust_median"],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current set has only 4 candidates with n_windows=3 (low). Universe shows exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0, ade_dynamic_error_beta0.3_trim1.0, and robust_median all achieve RMSE=18259.36 vs current best of 19376.16 (~6% improvement). Adding these top performers from universe leaderboards addresses the stability issue (rmse_std_over_rmse=0.45) and provides diversity. n_unique_winners=11 justifies keeping per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.109 < 0.25 so disagreement isn't extreme but still warrants weighted methods.",
  "changes": ["added exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0 for top RMSE/SMAPE/MAPE performance", "added ade_dynamic_error_beta0.3_trim1.0 for concept drift robustness", "added robust_median for stability with low n_windows"],
  "when_good": "When n_windows >= 6, ridge_stacking becomes viable to add."
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.47) and extreme seasonality variance (0.83), indicating models capture patterns very differently. Early horizon specialists (rf, NaiveSeasonal) differ from late horizon specialists (ARIMA), suggesting temporal pattern divergence. The DBA combination method is recommended to handle this heterogeneity while leveraging each model's unique strengths in trend tracking and seasonal decomposition.

proposer.rationale: Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.83) and model disagreement. n_unique_winners=11 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. With only n_windows=3, shrinkage=0.25 on inverse_rmse_weights provides stability. DBA handles phase-shifted predictions from early/late horizon specialists (rf vs ARIMA). Top-k mean with k=3 balances diversity and robustness.

statistician.rationale: Current set has only 4 candidates with n_windows=3 (low). Universe shows exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0, ade_dynamic_error_beta0.3_trim1.0, and robust_median all achieve RMSE=18259.36 vs current best of 19376.16 (~6% improvement). Adding these top performers from universe leaderboards addresses the stability issue (rmse_std_over_rmse=0.45) and provides diversity. n_unique_winners=11 justifies keeping per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.109 < 0.25 so disagreement isn't extreme but still warrants weighted methods.
changes: added exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0 for top RMSE/SMAPE/MAPE performance
added ade_dynamic_error_beta0.3_trim1.0 for concept drift robustness
added robust_median for stability with low n_windows
statistician.when_good: When n_windows >= 6, ridge_stacking becomes viable to add.
